company_profile.company_profile_text:
Who We Are
Meridian Intelligence GmbH is a Berlin-based research and data analytics consultancy specialising in market intelligence for EU institutional clients and large-scale private sector organisations. We help clients understand complex, fragmented markets — identifying who the relevant actors are, how they are distributed, how markets are evolving, and what the evidence base is for regulatory or strategic decisions.
We combine proprietary web-based data infrastructure with rigorous research methodology to produce market intelligence that goes beyond what commercial databases can provide. Our work is particularly suited to niche and fragmented markets where standard classification systems fail to capture the relevant population — sectors like cybersecurity, data services, AI, digital media, fintech, and advanced manufacturing.
Since 2016, we have delivered more than 40 completed projects for EU institutional clients, with a cumulative dataset volume exceeding 80 million organisational web records processed.

Our Services
Market and Ecosystem Mapping
We identify and profile all relevant organisations in a defined market — including actors that are invisible to standard databases because they are small, niche, or not self-labelled. Our mapping methodology combines large-scale web data collection, automated classification, and manual verification to produce structured, machine-readable entity datasets with full indicator profiles.
Continuous Market Monitoring
We maintain live monitoring capabilities for client-defined markets, delivering structured updates on new entrants, exits, portfolio changes, and ecosystem shifts. Update cadences range from weekly (for fast-moving markets) to quarterly (for stable sectors). All updates include structured change logs.
Regulatory and Policy Research
We support EU institutions and member state bodies in translating regulatory frameworks into empirical analytical scopes. This includes defining which entities fall within a regulation's coverage, operationalising legal definitions as classification criteria, and producing the evidence base for impact assessments and policy evaluations.
Supply Chain and Network Analysis
We map relationships between organisations in a defined ecosystem — supplier-customer relationships, subcontracting chains, ownership networks, and collaboration links. Our supply chain analysis is derived from web evidence and procurement data rather than self-reported survey responses.

Our Approach
The core of Meridian's methodology is what we call evidence-based organisation profiling: the systematic identification and classification of organisations using observable, verifiable signals rather than self-reported classifications or generic keyword matching.

We start with the broadest possible universe of candidates — typically all organisations with a web presence in the relevant geography.
We then apply a cascade of classification filters, each requiring a specific type of hard evidence to retain a candidate in scope.
We run automated quality checks and a manual verification pass on a statistical sample of each delivered dataset.
We track and publish our false-positive rate, coverage rate, and validation statistics with every major deliverable.

This approach consistently outperforms database-lookup methods in markets where the relevant actors are not centrally registered or do not self-identify with standard industry labels — which is the case in the majority of our clients' markets of interest.

Selected Clients

Key Facts for Tender Submissions

Our Methodology: WebMap
WebMap is Meridian's proprietary five-stage pipeline for large-scale organisational profiling. It differs from
database-lookup approaches in one fundamental respect: entities are included only if verifiable evidence
supports their relevance. We do not infer relevance from NACE codes or generic keywords.
Stage
What happens

1 — Seed universe
Ingest from company registries (Tier 1) + proprietary web index (Tier 2) + sector registries (Tier 3). EU27
coverage starting from 2–12M candidates.
2 — Deduplication
VAT-number matching → fuzzy name matching (Jaro-Winkler ≥ 0.92) → domain-level consolidation.
Duplicate rate post-dedup: <1.5%.
3 — Web data
collection
Crawl primary web domain. Extract text from About

Team pages. Archive with
timestamp.
4 — Evidence
classification
NLP-based classifier scores each candidate against scope criteria. Hard evidence required for inclusion.
Reject rate: typically 85–95% of seed universe.
5 — Verification &
profiling
Manual spot-check (5% random sample). Derive structured indicator profiles. False-positive rate in final
delivery: <3%.
Technology
Layer
Stack
Data collection
Scrapy

fine-tuned
BERT classifiers
Entity resolution
dedupe library + custom Jaro-Winkler
Pipeline orchestration
Apache Airflow
Storage & query
PostgreSQL

methodology_documents.description:
Overview
WebMap is Meridian Intelligence's proprietary framework for the systematic identification, classification, and continuous monitoring of organisations in defined markets. It has been developed iteratively since 2017 and applied in more than 25 completed projects covering markets ranging from cybersecurity SMEs to media publishers to advanced manufacturing firms across the EU27.
The core design principle of WebMap is evidence-first classification: organisations are retained in a defined market scope only if verifiable evidence supports their inclusion, not because they match a generic keyword or appear in a particular database. This produces lower headline counts than database-lookup approaches but substantially higher precision — typically validated false-positive rates of under 3%.

2.

Professional Summary: Economist and research director with 14 years of experience in market intelligence, policy-oriented empirical research, and EU institutional consulting. Leads Meridian's client portfolio across European Commission directorates, EU regulatory agencies, and public research institutes. Specialist in designing large-scale market mapping studies that combine quantitative data infrastructure with qualitative policy analysis. Native German speaker; working fluency in English and French.
Education: PhD in Economics — Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin (2011)
Dissertation: Structural Change and Knowledge Intensity in European Industry Clusters
Supervisors: Prof. Dr. Jens Hartig, Prof. Dr. Miriam Löhr
M.Sc. in European Economic Studies — College of Europe, Bruges (2007)
Specialisation: EU Internal Market and Regulatory Policy
B.Sc. in Economics — Freie Universität Berlin (2006)
Professional Experience: Managing Director — Meridian Intelligence GmbH, Berlin
2019 – present

Oversees all client-facing research operations and business development. Manages a team of 12 researchers and data analysts. Responsible for methodology design, quality assurance, and senior stakeholder engagement. Key accounts include DG CNECT, JRC, ENISA, EBA, and several large German industrial associations.
Selected project leadership:

ENISA/2023/OP/0008 — Cybersecurity SME Landscape in the EU: Population mapping, market sizing, and supply chain analysis across 27 member states. Delivered structured dataset of 14,200+ verified entities.
JRC/2024/OP/0019 — European Data Economy Monitoring Tool update: Led methodology redesign for web-based indicator derivation; supervised delivery of quarterly dataset updates.
EBA/2024/OP/0003 — FinTech and Digital Finance Market Analysis: Principal investigator for pan-EU market sizing study covering 6,400+ entities.
Senior Researcher — Prognos AG, Berlin

Led EU-funded research projects in industrial economics and labour market analysis. Primary client contacts at DG GROW and DG EMPL. Developed Prognos's first web-scraping-based company profiling methodology.
Research Associate — DIW Berlin (German Institute for Economic Research)
Contributed to panel data studies on firm-level innovation and productivity. Published three peer-reviewed papers on knowledge spillovers and regional industrial clusters.
Selected Publications: Becker, A. & Vogel, T. (2023). "Web-derived indicators for SME market mapping: methodology and validation." Journal of Industrial Economics and Data, 14(2), 88–107.
Becker, A. (2019). "Identifying niche market actors in fragmented EU industries: limits of standard databases." European Economic Review, 112, 201–218.
Becker, A. & Hartig, J. (2012). "Knowledge intensity and regional clustering in German manufacturing." Regional Studies, 46(8), 1033–1051.
sections.Role in Proposed Project: Project Director. Overall responsibility for delivery, client relationship management, and quality assurance. Will chair all steering committee meetings and sign off on all deliverables.

Professional Summary: Applied data scientist specialising in natural language processing, entity classification, and large-scale text analysis. Joined Meridian in 2021. Responsible for the design and maintenance of Meridian's NLP classification pipeline — the core engine that determines which organisations are relevant to a given market scope based on their web content. Particular expertise in fine-tuning transformer-based models for domain-specific classification tasks in German and English. Background in computational linguistics and applied ML research.
Education: M.Sc. in Computational Linguistics — Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München (2020)
Thesis: Domain adaptation for low-resource entity classification using web-scraped training data
B.Sc. in Informatics — Technische Universität München (2018)
Professional Experience: Data Scientist — Meridian Intelligence GmbH, Berlin
2021 – present
Owns the NLP and classification layer of Meridian's WebMap pipeline. Core responsibilities:
Design and training of multi-label text classifiers for organisational scope determination (currently in deployment: cybersecurity, data economy, AI/ML, fintech, industrial deep tech classifiers)
Entity resolution: hybrid blocking + embedding-based deduplication system processing 10M+ records
Model evaluation: stratified hold-out validation, false-positive auditing, quarterly retraining cycles
Research on classifier adaptation to new project scopes: typical time-to-deployment for a new domain classifier is 3–4 weeks
Notable technical contributions at Meridian:
Reduced WebMap classification latency by 60% by replacing spaCy rule-based pipelines with fine-tuned sentence-BERT models (2022)
Developed a cross-lingual entity matching module enabling reliable deduplication across 23 EU language variants (2023)

Built the AI involvement indicator introduced in JRC Phase III — a continuous score for AI-product-or-service relevance derived from web content, validated against a manually annotated sample of 4,200 companies (2024)
ML Research Assistant — Fraunhofer IAIS, Sankt Augustin

Contributed to a research project on automated knowledge graph construction from scientific literature. Developed named entity recognition models for scientific entity types (methods, datasets, metrics). Co-authored one peer-reviewed paper.
Technical Skills: NLP/ML: PyTorch, Transformers (Hugging Face), spaCy, scikit-learn, sentence-transformers, NLTK
Data infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Elasticsearch, Apache Airflow, Docker
Evaluation: MLflow, Weights & Biases, custom hold-out validation frameworks
Selected Publications: Weber, M. & Schuster, J. (2021). "Semi-supervised named entity recognition for scientific text using weak supervision." Proceedings of EMNLP 2021 Workshop on Scientific Document Understanding, 88–96.
sections.Role in Proposed Project: Data Science Lead. Responsible for classifier development and maintenance, entity resolution, model evaluation, and any AI/ML technical deliverables. Works directly under Thomas Vogel's technical oversight.

Professional Summary: Policy researcher and regulatory analyst with ten years of experience in EU digital and technology policy. Specialises in translating regulatory frameworks into analytical scopes for market intelligence projects — defining what needs to be measured, which entities are in scope, and what evidence constitutes relevance under a given legal definition. At Meridian since 2020. Prior experience at the European Parliament and a Brussels-based policy think tank. Fluent in four languages; frequent presenter at EU policy forums.
Education: M.A. in European Public Policy — Sciences Po Paris (2014)
Specialisation: Digital Governance and Technology Regulation
B.A. in Political Science and Economics — University of Edinburgh (2012)
Professional Experience: Research Lead, Policy & Regulatory Analysis — Meridian Intelligence GmbH, Berlin
2020 – present
Leads policy scoping and regulatory interpretation for all Meridian projects involving EU legislative frameworks. Responsibilities include:
Translating legal definitions (e.g., AI Act risk categories, DORA entity scope, NIS2 essential entities) into operational classification criteria for data collection
Authoring policy context sections of all major research deliverables
Managing stakeholder consultation processes, including expert interviews and advisory group facilitation
Co-leading business development for EU regulatory support projects
Selected contributions:
ENISA/2023/OP/0008: Developed the sector-by-sector scope definition for cybersecurity product categories, mapped to NACE codes and CRA product taxonomy
EBA/2024/OP/0003: Led regulatory scoping for DORA-relevant third-party ICT providers; designed the entity typology used for classification
DG CNECT/2023/OP/0021: Authored policy context analysis for European media market monitoring study
Policy Analyst — Brussels European and Global Economic Laboratory (BRUEGEL), Brussels

Produced policy briefs, working papers, and testimonies on EU digital single market regulation, platform economy governance, and data policy. Presented findings to European Parliament committees on three occasions. Managed a portfolio of 6–8 research projects simultaneously.
Parliamentary Assistant — European Parliament, Brussels

Supported MEP Renate Künast (Greens/EFA) on digital rights and data protection dossiers during the GDPR trilogue process. Drafted briefing notes, attended committee hearings, and coordinated with civil society organisations.
Selected Publications & Contributions: Chen, S. (2023). "Operationalising the AI Act: from legal text to market scope." Meridian Intelligence Policy Brief, No. 7.
Chen, S. & Becker, A. (2022). "Who is a cybersecurity provider? Classification challenges under the NIS2 Directive." European Cybersecurity Journal, 8(1), 44–59.
Chen, S. (2019). "Platform intermediaries and the limits of self-classification: evidence from EU e-commerce." BRUEGEL Working Paper 2019/07.
sections.Role in Proposed Project: Policy Lead. Responsible for regulatory scoping, entity typology design, policy context sections of all deliverables, and management of any stakeholder consultation activities. Will serve as day-to-day contact for the contracting authority on all substantive policy questions.

team_cvs.name: Thomas Vogel
team_cvs.title: Senior Data Engineer & Web Intelligence Lead

Professional Summary: Data engineer and applied researcher specialising in large-scale web data acquisition, natural language processing, and automated entity classification. At Meridian since 2017. Designed and maintains the firm's core web intelligence pipeline — a system capable of ingesting, processing, and classifying organisational web data at EU-wide scale. Strong background in Python, cloud infrastructure, and reproducible data workflows. Comfortable bridging technical implementation and research methodology.
Education: M.Sc. in Data Science — Technische Universität Berlin (2016)
Thesis: Automated classification of firm-level technological capabilities using web text data
B.Sc. in Computer Science — Karlsruher Institut für Technologie (2014)
Professional Experience: Senior Data Engineer — Meridian Intelligence GmbH, Berlin
2017 – present
Leads all technical infrastructure for Meridian's data collection and processing pipeline. Core responsibilities include:
Design and maintenance of web crawling, text extraction, and deduplication systems covering 30M+ organisational web pages
Development of NLP-based classification models for entity profiling (sector, capabilities, size proxies)
Delivery of structured datasets in machine-readable formats for EU institutional clients
Technical supervision of two junior data engineers
Selected technical contributions:
Built Meridian's core entity resolution system, reducing duplicate-organisation rate from ~12% to under 1.5% across EU-wide datasets
Developed automated pipeline for quarterly dataset refresh, reducing manual processing time by ~70%
Led technical delivery for JRC/2024/OP/0019, including API wrapper for near-real-time indicator access
Junior Data Analyst — Bertelsmann Stiftung, Gütersloh
Supported quantitative research projects on European labour market dynamics. Built data pipelines for longitudinal panel datasets from Eurostat and national statistical offices.
Technical Skills: Frameworks: Pandas, spaCy, scikit-learn, FastAPI, Apache Airflow
Infrastructure: AWS (S3, EC2, Lambda), Docker, PostgreSQL, Elasticsearch
Data formats: JSON, Parquet, CSV, XML, REST APIs
Selected Project Contributions: ENISA/2023/OP/0008 - Technical lead: EU cybersecurity entity dataset (14,200+ records, quarterly refresh)
JRC/2024/OP/0019 - Pipeline architect: Web indicator derivation system; API-delivered updates
EBA/2024/OP/0003 - Data engineering: FinTech entity classification model (F1: 0.87 on held-out validation set)
DG GROW/2022/OP/0011 - Data analyst: Automated supply chain relationship extraction from procurement records
sections.Role in Proposed Project: Technical Lead. Responsible for all data infrastructure, pipeline development, entity resolution, and machine-readable deliverable production. Point of contact for all technical queries from the contracting authority.
